description: Legacy flat network - worst security practice, no segmentation, mesh connectivity

expected_attack_paths.rationale: Direct path from Internet to Database through router with no firewall
Direct access to file server from Internet
Workstations directly exposed to Internet, can pivot to other resources
Lateral movement from compromised workstation through file server to database
Direct connection between web server and database (common in flat networks)

ransomware.rationale: No segmentation, no EDR, no backup visible. Everything is one hop from Internet.

application_vulns.rationale: No WAF, no firewall, direct Internet exposure of all systems

phishing.rationale: Workstations exposed, no MFA, no email gateway

insider_threat.rationale: No access logging, no segmentation, workstations can reach everything

dos.rationale: Single router, no load balancing, no DDoS protection

supply_chain.rationale: Flat network means compromised vendor access = full access

rationale: Worst-case legacy architecture: Flat network with no segmentation, no firewall on router, everything directly accessible from Internet, mesh connectivity allows unlimited lateral movement. This is a textbook example of what NOT to do.
